description: Perform a multilevel linear model analysis of math achievement using the High School and Beyond (HSB) datasets (`nlme::MathAchieve` and `nlme::MathAchSchool`) to investigate school effects. The analysis requires fitting a Null Model (random intercept only), a Student Model (fixed effects for `ses`, `minority`, and `sex`), and a Contextual Model (adding school-level fixed effects `Sector` and `MEANSES`). Record the analysis in a script at `/home/ga/RProjects/hsb_multilevel.R`. Export the following results to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/`: a model comparison table (`hsb_model_comparison.csv`) including `model`, `n_fixed`, `AIC`, `BIC`, `loglik`, and the Null Model ICC; a fixed effects table for the Contextual Model (`hsb_fixed_effects.csv`) with `term`, `estimate`, `std_error`, and `t_value`; a caterpillar plot of the 160 school random intercepts with 95% prediction intervals sorted by value (`hsb_caterpillar.png`); and a visualization of the SES-Math relationship (`hsb_ses_effects.png`).
